text:
Admission Date:  1986-10-16              Discharge Date:   2017-05-08

Date of Birth:  1989-11-16             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
No Allergies/ADRs on File

Attending:Robert
Chief Complaint:
Ventricular fibrillation arrest

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Coronary artery catheterization with placement of a drug eluting
stent

History of Present Illness:
65 year-old man with a h/o CAD s/p MI 21yrs ago, cath w/o
intervention in 2004, HTN, HLD, ED who is transferred from the
Barbourville Arh Hospital VA s/p VT/VF arrest. The patient reported a couple
episodes of lightheadedness during exercise to his PCP last
week.  ECG showed no acute findings at the time, but he was
advised to undergo exercise stress testing, which he already had
scheduled for today as part of his compensation application due
to Vasquez USCGC Garcia
FPO AA 86965-1288 exposure.  He walked for 8.5min (10 METS) on a
Steven protocol when he developed chest discomfort/SOB for which
stress was stopped. At 6 minutes of exercise, he reportedly had
1mm ST depressions in V5-V6. 1 minute into recovery, he
complained of lightheadedness.  His rhythm was noted on
telemetry to be VT which degenerated into VF.  Code blue was
called at 9:12:52, and CPR was initiated with delivery of one
200J shock at 9:15am and return to spontaneous circulation after
one minute.  BP 140/80; pt awake and talking.  He was
transferred to the ED where he had a 10-beat run of asymptomatic
polymorphic VT.  He was started on IVF, given metoprolol 5mg IV
as he skipped his AM coreg, given an 150mg amiodarone bolus then
bolus, and given Mg 2gm IV.  He was then transferred to the
Cedar Ridge ED.  Of note, stress in 04/1994 showed large inferoapical
hypokinesis with minimal peri-infarction ischemia and with an EF
of 39%.
.
VS on ED arrival: T 98.1, HR 68, 141/89, RR 18, 99%.  Pt was
pain free and asymptomatic.  His exam was benign.  EKG showed
NSR 67 with NI/NA, Q in III, aVF.  CXR showed no acute
cardiopulmonary abnormalities or definite displaced rib
fractures.  Labs unremarkable with negative trop x 1.  The
patient was continued on the amiodarone gtt and remained
hemodynamically stable in sinus rhythm.  He is being admitted to
the CCU for monitoring in anticipation of cardiac cath tomorrow.
 On transfer, T 97.1, P 61, 97% on 2L, BP 152/71.
.
On review of systems, he denies any prior history of stroke,
TIA, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding at the
time of surgery, myalgias, joint pains, cough, hemoptysis, black
stools or red stools. He denies recent fevers, chills or rigors.
He denies exertional buttock or calf pain. All of the other
review of systems were negative.
.
Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of chest pain
although he does describe a mild persistsent burning sensation
over his lower left chest/LUQ x several days; unchanged by
exertion, position, or diet; not consistent with heartburn.  Of
note, his anginal equivalent with his MI was a sensation of
throat fullness, which he has not experienced recently.  He has
had gradually worsening dyspnea on exertion in the past several
months to year.  Occasional LE edema which improves with lower
extremity elevation.  Denies syncope.


Past Medical History:
1. CARDIAC RISK FACTORS: -Diabetes, +Dyslipidemia, +Hypertension

2. CARDIAC HISTORY:  CAD s/p MI [**54**] years ago
-CABG:  None
-PERCUTANEOUS CORONARY INTERVENTIONS: "3 vessel POBA" per pt 21
years ago.  Repeat cath in 2004 with "open vessels" per pt.
-PACING/ICD:  None.
3. OTHER PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
Erectile dysfunction
S/p radical prostatectomy 01/1986


Social History:
-Tobacco history:  Quit smoking in 2004 after MI. 60 pack year
hx.
-ETOH:  Occasional. Last 1 week ago.
-Illicit drugs: denies
Drives HAZMAT truck for Exxon. Lives in 09505 Chen Well
Brendaland, NE 43656 with wife in
house. Last exercise Monday, treadmill x 30min, bike x 20min.


Family History:
Father died of MI at age 59.  No family history of arrhythmia,
cardiomyopathies, or sudden cardiac death; otherwise
non-contributory.

Physical Exam:
On admission
PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
VS: T=97.3 BP=146/83 HR=72 RR=15 O2 sat=96% RA
GENERAL: WDWN Caucasian male in NAD. Oriented x3. Mood, affect
appropriate.
HEENT: NCAT. Sclera anicteric. PERRL, EOMI. Conjunctiva were
pink, no pallor or cyanosis of the oral mucosa. No xanthalesma.

NECK: Supple with no JVD.
CARDIAC: PMI located in 5th intercostal space, midclavicular
line. RR, normal S1, S2. No m/r/g. No thrills, lifts. No S3 or
S4.
LUNGS: No chest wall deformities, scoliosis or kyphosis. Resp
were unlabored, no accessory muscle use. CTAB, no crackles,
wheezes or rhonchi.
ABDOMEN: Soft, obese, NTND. No HSM or tenderness. Abd aorta not
enlarged by palpation. No abdominal bruits.
EXTREMITIES: No c/c/e. No femoral bruits.
SKIN: No stasis dermatitis, ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.
PULSES:
Right: Carotid 2+ Femoral 1+ DP 2+ PT 2+
Left: Carotid 2+ Femoral 1+  DP 2+ PT 2+
.

HEENT: NCAT. Sclera anicteric. PERRL, EOMI. Conjunctiva were

CARDIAC: PMI located in 5th intercostal space, midclavicular

LUNGS: No chest wall deformities, scoliosis or kyphosis. Resp

ABDOMEN: Soft, obese, NTND. No HSM or tenderness. Abd aorta not

EXTREMITIES: No c/c/e. No femoral bruits.
SKIN: No stasis dermatitis, ulcers, scars, or xanthomas.
PULSES:

Left: Carotid 2+ Femoral 1+  DP 2+ PT 2+

MCV-90 MCH-32.2* MCHC-36.0* RDW-13.0 Plt Ct-164

K-3.5 Cl-106 HCO3-24 AnGap-14

MCV-88 MCH-31.2 MCHC-35.7* RDW-13.3 Plt Ct-146*
2017-05-08 05:40AM BLOOD Glucose-104* UreaN-16 Creat-1.0 Na-139
K-4.5 Cl-106 HCO3-25 AnGap-13
2017-05-08 05:40AM BLOOD CK-MB-3
2017-05-08 05:40AM BLOOD Calcium-9.0 Phos-2.8 Mg-2.3

===========================Imaging===========================
OSH 1988-09-24 Exercise stress echo:  Excellent functional capacity
(10 minutes on Veronica protocol).  Preserved LV systolic function
with ejection fraction at 50-55%.  No evidence of significant
ischemic burden.  Not significantly changed from one year ago.
.
OSH 1983-12-23 Adenosine exercise stress test: Technically good
study. Large fixed inferoapical defect, consistent with prior
infarction with minimal peri-infarction ischemia.  LV
contractility 39% with inferoapical hypokinesis.
.
ECHO 2009-11-07
Suboptimal image quality. The left atrium is mildly dilated.
Left ventricular wall thicknesses are normal. The left
ventricular cavity size is normal. There is probably moderate
regional left ventricular systolic dysfunction with inferior,
anteroseptal and apical hypokinesis. No masses or thrombi are
seen in the left ventricle. There is no ventricular septal
defect. The aortic valve leaflets are mildly thickened (?#).
There is no aortic valve stenosis. No aortic regurgitation is
seen. Trivial mitral regurgitation is seen. The left ventricular
inflow pattern suggests impaired relaxation. There is no
pericardial effusion.
.
EKG 2009-11-07
Sinus rhythm. The P-R interval is prolonged. The Q-T interval is
prolonged. There are non-diagnostic Q waves in the inferior
leads consistent with probable prior myocardial infarction.
Compared to the previous tracing of 1986-10-16 the P-R interval
and Q-T intervals are longer.


Brief Hospital Course:
65yo M w/ CAD s/p distant MI, HTN, HLD who presents after VF
arrest during recovery from exercise stress test.
.
# Ventricular fibrillation arrest: Patient had witnessed VF
arrest at VA during recovery period of stress test. According to
the report, CPR was initiated immediately ROSC after 1 shock
given <3 minutes after code blue called. Electrophysiology was
consulted who identified VF arrest as related to ischemia (see
below) and recommended discontinuation of amiodarone. He was
discharged with a plan to follow up with his cardiologist Dr.
Barrett.
.
# CORONARIES: Patient has known CAD with last cath in 2004
without intervention per patient report. On admission to Cedar Ridge,
he was taken for cardiac catheritization which showed CTO of RCA
and culprit lesion in the Diagonal artery which was stented with
DES. He was started on Prasugrel 10mg daily which he will need
to take for at least one year. Home medications were continued:
aspirin 325mg daily, rosuvastatin 10mg daily, carvedilol 25mg
Sparks Regional Medical Center, and amlodipine 5mg Sparks Regional Medical Center.
.
# PUMP: Pt. with estimated LVEF of 40% on 04/1994 stress test. No
s/s of volume overload at this time. TTE in this admission
showed LVEF 35%, and moderate regional left ventricular systolic
dysfunction with inferior, anteroseptal and apical hypokinesis.
He was discharged with a plan for out patient follow up with his
cardiologist, Dr. Barrett, and repeat echo in 3 months followed
by possible ICD placement if EF remains depressed. Home
medications were continued:carvedilol as above and  lisinopril
20mg Sparks Regional Medical Center.
.
# CODE: Confirmed full


Medications on Admission:
ASA 325mg daily
Rosuvastatin 10mg daily
Carvedilol 25mg Sparks Regional Medical Center
Lisinopril 20mg Sparks Regional Medical Center
Amlodipine 5mg Sparks Regional Medical Center

Discharge Medications:
1. rosuvastatin 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO once a day.

2. carvedilol 25 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO twice a day.
3. amlodipine 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
4. lisinopril 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day).
5. aspirin 325 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
6. prasugrel 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*3*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
Primary diagnosis:
Acute coronary ischmia
Polymorphic ventricular tachycardia

Secondary diagnosis:
Dyslipidemia
Hypertension


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent.

Discharge Instructions:
Mr. Roberson,
 As you know, you were admitted to Cedar Ridge for evaluation of an
arrhythmia during an exercise stress test.  You were found to
have a blockage in one of your coronary arteries that required
stenting to reopen the artery. You did not have any subsequent
arrhythmias during your hospitalization.
 When you go home, it is very important that you continue to
take Aspirin and Prasugrel, these medications help to prevent
blood clots from forming in the stents that were placed in this
hospitalization. You need to take Prasugrel for a minimum of one
year. Do not stop taking these medications unless directed by a
cardiologist.
 We recommend that you avoid strenuous exercise or heavy lifting
until approved by your cardiologist.
While you were in the hospital, we examined your heart using an
ultrasound (echocardiogram)and noted that your heart is not
pumping as efficiently as it should. This may improve over the
next three to six months.  You will need a repeat echocardiogram
in 3 months. If your heart function is decreased then, you may
require an implantable defibrillator. In the meantime, weigh
yourself daily in the morning after urinating, if you gain more
than 2 lbs in one day or more than 3 lbs in a week, please
contact your primary care provider.

MEDICATION CHANGES:
START Prasugrel 10mg daily (DO NOT STOP THIS MEDICATION FOR ANY
REASON without discussing with your cardiologist first)


Followup Instructions:
Please contact your cardiologist, Dr. Barrett (376-506-1601), to
arrange a followup appointment within 2 weeks.
